active_themes.label: Warsh Confirmation: Independence Rhetoric Meets Pressure Reality
active_themes.summary: Warsh testified April 21 maintaining Fed independence commitment while refusing to commit to current rate-cut stance. No hard blocking votes yet. What changed today: the emerging picture is that Warsh's eventual confirmation—likely to proceed—will be interpreted by markets as a capitulation point, not a leadership transition. If confirmed while Powell simultaneously faces Trump pressure, the market reprices not just monetary policy but Fed credibility itself. The timing of Warsh's confirmation against the Iran crisis backdrop makes this a dual-legitimacy test the Fed may fail.

active_themes.label: Iran Ceasefire: Breathing Room, Not Resolution
active_themes.summary: US announced ceasefire extension today; oil traders continue pricing assumption that talks restart before supply destruction becomes irreversible. Real economic damage visible (airline guidance cuts, CO2 contingency planning in UK) but contained. The open question remains unchanged: whether actual supply losses force the market to reprice blockade duration from 3 weeks to 3-6 months. Ceasefire extension bought time but did not resolve the underlying tension or explain Iran's incentive to extend talks rather than escalate.

active_themes.label: Norm-Breaking as Structural Feature
active_themes.summary: Pattern deepens: Warsh confirmation occurs while Powell faces investigation-by-pressure; DOJ investigating SPLC; Navy Secretary Phelan fired today; Sullivan & Cromwell apologizing for AI hallucinations in legal filings. This is not isolated scandal—it is an operating model where institutional decisions are made under informal threat of consequence. Markets have not yet priced the cost of decisions being made for political rather than economic reasons during dual crisis (Fed succession + geopolitical risk). The rally today masks the fact that institutional decay is accelerating at precisely the moment when institutional credibility is most needed.

open_watchlist.condition: Powell makes any public statement acknowledging Trump's May timeline or hinting at stepping down
open_watchlist.meaning: Fed independence has cracked publicly; market reprices regime change risk and Warsh's confirmation odds spike sharply upward.

open_watchlist.condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises above 4.2% while S&P stays flat or down
open_watchlist.meaning: Fed expectations are decoupling from equity valuations—bond market is pricing stagflation faster than stocks; sell signal for duration and growth.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms oil traders' bet that talks restart before supply destruction becomes irreversible; keeps oil below $95 and reduces tail risk of 3-6 month blockade.

open_watchlist.condition: WTI oil closes above $95 for two consecutive trading sessions
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market repricing of Iran supply crisis duration or severity; forces simultaneous reassessment of Fed independence risk if Warsh confirms at same time oil is spiking.

open_watchlist.condition: Fed official (Powell, governor, or staff) makes public statement on rates that sounds politically motivated rather than data-driven
open_watchlist.meaning: First explicit signal that institutional capture has moved from theory to practice; would likely trigger equities sell-off and 10-year yield spike.

open_watchlist.condition: Senate Democrats signal willingness to vote against Warsh confirmation (not delay, but actual opposition votes above 10 senators)
open_watchlist.meaning: Would slow timeline and force Warsh to make explicit commitments on Fed independence; could delay confirmation into June and create window where market reprices the regime shift risk.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation with specific names and mandate statement within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms talks are moving from ceasefire extension to actual negotiation; opens door to 3-6 week resolution timeline instead of indefinite blockade.

open_watchlist.condition: WTI oil closes above $100 for two consecutive trading days
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market no longer believes Iran talks will hold; reprices blockade duration from temporary to structural; forces stagflation repricing in equities.

open_watchlist.condition: Powell makes public statement acknowledging Trump's May timeline or hinting at stepping down
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms Fed leadership is being rewritten under political pressure during active crisis; market reprices institutional credibility and Fed independence premium disappears.

open_watchlist.condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises above 4.2% while S&P 500 stays flat or declines
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market is pricing inflation or institutional risk premium (not growth), indicating dual crisis is being repriced as stagflation rather than contained geopolitical shock.
